channel.title: weekly on Martin Proks (@mproksik)
channel.description: Recent content in weekly on Martin Proks (@mproksik)

item.title: Weekly news #7
item.description: News Stanford Makes iPhone Development Course Free Rust gets its own foundation Science/Bioinformatics The next 20 years of human genomics must be more equitable and more open DOI: 10.1038/d41586-021-00328-0 Brain2Pix: Fully convolutional naturalistic video reconstruction from brain activity Le et al., biorvix 2021 #neural-network #fmri #image-reconstruction Reconstructing complex and dynamic visual perception from brain activity remains a major challenge in machine learning applications to neuroscience. Here we present a new method for reconstructing naturalistic images and videos from very large single-participant functional magnetic resonance data that leverages the recent success of image-to-image transformation networks.

item.title: Weekly news #6
item.description: News explosion/spaCy v3 pip dropping support for python 2 Shiny 1.6 dplyr 1.0.4 homebrew 3.0 Science/Bioinformatics Initialization is critical for preserving global data structure in both t-SNE and UMAP Kobak et al., 2021 Nat Biotechnol #scrna-seq #dimension-reduction Five reasons why researchers should learn to love the command line Jeffrey M. Perkel #command-line The text interface is intimidating, but can save researchers from mundane computing tasks. Just be sure you know what you’re doing.

item.title: Weekly news #5
item.description: News Announcing the 2020 Golden Kitty Award Winners Postgres broken on Mac OS Big Sur Science/Bioinformatics Seven myths of how transcription factors read the cis-regulatory code Zeitlinger et al., Current Opinion 2021 #transcription-factors #enhancers Genomics data are now being generated at large quantities, of exquisite high resolution and from single cells. They offer a unique opportunity to develop powerful machine learning algorithms, including neural networks, to uncover the rules of the cis-regulatory code.

item.title: Weekly news #4
item.description: News RStudio 1.4 is out 🎉 Raspberry Pi Pico now on sale at $4 IPFS Support in Brave browser Science/Bioinformatics Ten computer codes that transformed science Jeffrey M. Perkel From Fortran to arXiv.org, these advances in programming and platforms sent biology, climate science and physics into warp speed. Noninvasive Precision Screening of Prostate Cancer by Urinary Multimarker Sensor and Artificial Intelligence Analysis Hojun et al., ACS Nano, 2021

item.title: Weekly news #3
item.description: News Nature Computational Science issue #1 is out 🎉 Pirated Academic Database Sci-Hub Is Now on the ‘Uncensorable Web’ Science/Bioinformatics Lessons from the COVID-19 pandemic for advancing computational drug repurposing strategies Galindez et al., Nat Comput Sci, 2021 #covid-19 #drug-disvocery Responding quickly to unknown pathogens is crucial to stop uncontrolled spread of diseases that lead to epidemics, such as the novel coronavirus, and to keep protective measures at a level that causes as little social and economic harm as possible.

item.title: Weekly news #2
item.description: Science/Bioinformatics DeepCell Kiosk: scaling deep learning–enabled cellular image analysis with Kubernetes Bannon et al., Nat Methods, 2021 #image-analysis #kubernetes #google-cloud 📋New software DeepCell Kiosk is designed to scale predefined deep-learning image analysis on Google cloud using Kubernetes. Deep learning is transforming the analysis of biological images, but applying these models to large datasets remains challenging. Here we describe the DeepCell Kiosk, cloud-native software that dynamically scales deep learning workflows to accommodate large imaging datasets.

item.title: Weekly news #1
item.description: Design patterns using Rust Are you a Rust fan? Did you know that there is an open source book where people contribute their code on how to implement various design patterns in Rust? Go check it out, the community already reached 4.2k stars on GitHub! Docker best practices We all use docker these days, but “with great power comes great responsibility” - Uncle Ben. Do you follow some security rules when you write your own Dockerfile?

item.title: Weekly news #
item.description: News Science/Bioinformatics et al., # Abstract Programming Lorem impus Tools Lorem impus Guides and Tutorials Lorem impus Others Did you know?
